verdict_reason: BG 2.10 is narrative; no soteriologically load-bearing technical term is in play. The engineering layer correctly preserves the iva (as if) qualifier on prahasann — 'smiling \"as if\" — not amused, but settled' — which is the doctrinally important particle that all major commentators read carefully. No tradition is conflated; the engineering layer takes the cross-traditional consensus reading (the smile is settled-teacher's readiness, not amusement) and stays inside it. The geometry of the verse (between the two armies) is preserved without overload of doctrinal commitment. PASS.
